solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact string 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' (test_padded_pod_array_storage), exact string 'pad_string.insert(pad_string_.begin(), pad_string_.end())' (test_insert_from_itself_usage), exact string 'chassert(column_pad_const)' (test_chassert_used_after_validation), 'const ColumnsWithTypeAndName & arguments' (test_function_signature_updated). tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test reads the source file and checks for literal string patterns using 'in content' or regex matches. No test compiles or executes the C++ code, runs the ClickHouse functions, or verifies actual runtime behavior. This is pure text matching, e.g., test_padded_pod_array_storage checks 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' in content.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since tests only check file content for specific strings, an agent could simply write the exact expected strings into the file without understanding the bug. For example, adding 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' as a comment would pass test_padded_pod_array_storage. The tests don't verify the code compiles or runs correctly.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/ClickHouse as solve.sh would apply the patch there. The agent could read the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to see exact expected strings (e.g., 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string', 'insertFromItself', etc.) and just insert those strings into the source file. The tests are pure text matching so matching literal fixture strings without understanding the bug would work.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction.md requirement 9 (Allman brace style) is mentioned but not tested. The testing section mentions testing with 'Long pad strings', 'UTF8 pad strings', 'Empty pad strings', and 'Various combinations' but no runtime tests exist for these.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive names (test_padded_pod_array_storage, test_insert_from_itself_usage, etc.), docstrings explaining the behavioral rationale, and clear FAIL-TO-PASS / PASS-TO-PASS sections.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves modifying C++ source code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No apparent typos in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in instruction.md or tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact file (src/Functions/padString.cpp), exact class (PaddingChars), exact member variable (pad_string), and prescribes exact replacement types and methods (PaddedPODArray, insertFromItself, chassert, validateFunctionArguments). This goes far beyond identifying the buggy module.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agent config edit task.
